nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Practical completion is a significant milestone in the construction industry
nodes.description: Practical completion signifies the end of the construction process and indicates when the works under the contract have been completed to the extent that the owner can occupy and use the building for its intended purpose.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Practical completion is defined as the point when all building works are completed except for minor defects
nodes.description: At practical completion, the responsibility for the building passes from the builder to the owner, allowing the owner to take possession of the property.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Reaching practical completion is essential for property developers
nodes.description: It marks the end of the construction phase, determines when the defects liability period starts, and triggers the commencement of the statutory warranty period and insurance under the Home Building Compensation Fund (HBCF).

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Practical completion allows developers to start marketing and selling their properties
nodes.description: This is essential for their business operations and financial management.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Failing to meet practical completion can result in liquidated damages for the builder
nodes.description: Liquidated damages are a predetermined amount of money that the builder can claim for each day that the project is delayed beyond the agreed practical completion date.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Failing to meet practical completion can delay the commencement of the warranty period and insurance coverage
nodes.description: This exposes developers to the risk of having to pay for repairs to any defects that arise during this time.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Developers should engage a reputable and experienced builder to ensure practical completion is met
nodes.description: This is one of the practical tips for developers to ensure that practical completion is achieved on time and to the required standards.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Developers should regularly inspect the work during construction to ensure it meets the required standards
nodes.description: This is another practical tip for ensuring that practical completion is met.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Practical completion is crucial for property developers to manage risks and liabilities
nodes.description: It plays a crucial role in mitigating risks and liabilities associated with building projects.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20 06:07] Practical completion triggers the commencement of the statutory warranty period under the Home Building Act 1989
nodes.description: This is important for ensuring that the property is covered by insurance and warranty.
